entrypoint.best_current_role: Client-side refresh or apply path that validates incoming launcher/config data, repopulates client +0x19A20 through 0x140033F90, and then either updates local mirror fields or falls back to opening a launcher URL.

precheck.bounded_role: Pre-validator for the incoming input bundle before the embedded object at client +0x19A20 is repopulated.

population.observation: The entrypoint passes lea [client +0x19A20] to 0x140033F90, which is the already-mapped parser that updates the embedded config object.

success_and_failure_split.observation: When the parse/apply result byte in dil is zero, the helper calls an imported open-like routine with the launcher URL string rooted at 0x140B5DFC0 and the verb string 0x140B5E008 = "open".
success_and_failure_split.bounded_inference: This is a launcher or shell fallback that opens the rs-launch jav_config.ws URL when the local refresh/apply path does not succeed.

success_and_failure_split.observation: When the parse/apply result byte is nonzero, the helper copies a small structured record into client +0x19E98 and then mirrors the game-variant display-name string from client +0x19A58 into client +0x19EA8.

copy_helper.observation: The helper clones a compact metadata record rather than reusing the source in place.

new_game_variant_display_name_consumer.observation: The helper loads client +0x19A58, dereferences descriptor +0x8, treats that pointer as a NUL-terminated string, and copies it into the client string slot at +0x19EA8 through 0x140031B10.
new_game_variant_display_name_consumer.updated_role: Concrete consumer of the game-variant display-name field, distinct from the previously mapped slug consumer at descriptor +0x10.

adjacent_string_mirrors.observation: A string-like field is mirrored through 0x140031B10 before the success/failure split.
A second string-like field is mirrored through 0x140031B10 before the success/failure split.

preserved_points: Client +0x19A20 remains the embedded parsed config object updated by 0x140033F90.
Client +0x19A58 remains the game-variant descriptor pointer rooted at 0x140C41510.
Descriptor +0x8 remains the display-name pointer and descriptor +0x10 remains the slug pointer.
new_points: The display-name field at descriptor +0x8 now has a concrete client mirror consumer at 0x14001F8AE..0x14001F8E0.
One 0x19A20 refresh path is now tied directly to a jav_config.ws rs-launch URL fallback when local parse/apply does not succeed.

updated_conclusion: A concrete refresh path around client +0x19A20 is now mapped as a launcher/config application flow. Helper 0x14001F7B0 first validates the incoming bundle through 0x14001F900, then repopulates the embedded object at client +0x19A20 via 0x140033F90. If that refresh/apply step fails, the client falls back to opening the literal rs-launch URL for jav_config.ws with the verb "open". If it succeeds, the client updates two local mirror strings, clones a compact metadata record from client +0x187D8 into client +0x19E98, and copies the game-variant display name from client +0x19A58 descriptor +0x8 into client +0x19EA8. This gives the game-variant descriptor a new concrete UI/launcher-facing consumer and ties one 0x19A20 update path directly to launcher configuration behavior.
impact_on_prior_artifacts: The game-variant-table artifact is strengthened by a new concrete display-name consumer at client +0x19EA8, complementing the earlier slug consumer and packet-byte consumer.
The embedded-locale-config-object artifact gains a concrete high-level refresh/apply path rather than only isolated field writes and abstract ownership.
Future work on the unresolved scalar at client +0x19A4C should consider both launcher/config code and local mirror-update paths, not just bootstrap packet builders.
next_targets: Find the first direct consumer of client +0x19A4C and determine whether it belongs to the same launcher/config family or a separate bootstrap-only branch.
Recover the exact semantics of the client +0x19E58, +0x19E70, and +0x19E98 mirror fields that are updated around this refresh path.
Determine whether the launcher URL fallback is purely error handling or a deliberate online refresh path that feeds the same parsed 0x19A20 bundle on the next pass.
